nativeRange.habitat: The Red Flame Sword is an aquarium-trade hybrid with no wild population of its own. Its parent species in the genus Echinodorus are native to tropical and subtropical marshes, riverbanks, and seasonally flooded wetlands across the Americas.

summary: The Red Flame Sword is an aquarium-trade hybrid of the Echinodorus genus, prized for its broad oval leaves marked with striking red veining and marbling. It is generally considered one of the easier rosette plants to keep and works well as a background or midground centerpiece in planted aquariums.
careNotes: Echinodorus 'Red Flame' is a HYBRID Echinodorus cultivar developed for the aquarium trade rather than a recognised wild species — exact parentage is not consistently published, which is why the scientific name remains informal as Echinodorus sp. 'Red Flame'. The broader Echinodorus genus is native to the Americas. Adult height is reported around 20–30 cm, making this plant noticeably shorter and broader-leaved than the classic Amazon Sword; in larger tanks it works as a background plant, and in mid-sized tanks it serves well as a midground centerpiece. Like other Echinodorus, the Red Flame Sword is a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER — a nutrient-rich substrate or regular root-tab supplementation is essentially required. Iron-rich fertilization is consistently recommended for developing strong red veining; without adequate iron, leaves tend to stay mostly green. Temperature tolerance is broad at 20–28 °C, with pH 6.0–7.5 and gH 4–18 well tolerated. Newly purchased plants often arrive with emersed-form leaves — broader and rounder than the submerged growth — that may die back as submerged leaves replace them. This transition is normal for Echinodorus and the plant generally recovers once it adjusts to submerged conditions.
breedingNotes: The Red Flame Sword propagates in the same way as other Echinodorus: adventitious plantlets (small daughter plants) form on emergent flower stalks and along runners or side shoots. Once a plantlet has developed a small rosette and visible roots, it can be separated from the parent and planted in the substrate. Crown division of mature plants is also possible. Flowering is uncommon when the plant is kept fully submerged under typical aquarium conditions.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Primary care-guide source for Echinodorus sp. 'Red Flame'. Confirmed common name Red Flame Sword, height ~12 in (~30 cm), temperature 68–82 °F (20–28 °C), moderate light, CO2 optional, easy care level, background/midground placement, runner/daughter-plant propagation, nutrient-rich substrate with root tabs, iron-rich fertilization for red coloration.

additional.name: Buce Plant — Echinodorus Red Flame
additional.notes: Cross-checked: family Alismataceae, height 8–12 in, pH 6.0–7.5, low light demand, slow-to-moderate growth, easy care, runner-plantlet propagation, root tabs recommended in inert substrate. Listing's stated origin 'South Africa' is treated as an error — Echinodorus is an exclusively American genus per Wikipedia.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Echinodorus
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (family Alismataceae, order Alismatales) and confirmation that the Echinodorus genus is native to the Americas, not Africa; notes that many aquarium-trade Echinodorus forms exist as named cultivars without formal subspecific status.
